function.content:
---
record_type: bill
bill_id: bill.house_mesh_juniper_ssd
alias: house_mesh_juniper_ssd
purchased_on: 2026-02-07
total_eur: 105
counterparty: Datenspeicher Köhler GmbH
project: House Mesh
related_entity: Juniper
lines:
  - item: 2 TB SATA SSD
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 89
    line_eur: 89
  - item: USB clone adapter
    quantity: 1
    unit_eur: 16
    line_eur: 16
---
# Juniper storage refresh

```text
+----------------+-----------------------------+
| field          | value                       |
+----------------+-----------------------------+
| record_type    | bill                        |
| bill_id        | bill.house_mesh_juniper_ssd |
| alias          | house_mesh_juniper_ssd      |
| purchased_on   | 2026-02-07                  |
| total_eur      | 105                         |
| counterparty   | Datenspeicher Köhler GmbH   |
| project        | House Mesh                  |
| related_entity | Juniper                     |
+----------------+-----------------------------+
```

## Line Items

```text
+---+-------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| # | item              | qty | unit_eur | line_eur |
+---+-------------------+-----+----------+----------+
| 1 | 2 TB SATA SSD     | 1   | 89       | 89       |
| 2 | USB clone adapter | 1   | 16       | 16       |
+---+-------------------+-----+----------+----------+
|   | TOTAL             |     |          | 105      |
+---+-------------------+-----+----------+----------+
```

## Notes

Part boring maintenance, part trust-building because Juniper backups were becoming real household infrastructure.

function.message: Done. I processed the next inbox request by OCR-structuring all Juniper-related bills found in finance records. One matching bill was found and updated with YAML frontmatter per the finance schema, preserving the original note body. The inbox item was then removed.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed mandatory bootstrap (tree/read AGENTS/context), read relevant nested AGENTS and 99_system workflow/schema docs before task work, preserved full original bill body during frontmatter migration, and deleted inbox file only after successful completion.
function.outcome_justification: Inbox request explicitly asked to OCR all bills related to Juniper. Search across /50_finance found one Juniper-related bill file (50_finance/purchases/2026_02_07__eur_000105__bill__house_mesh_juniper_ssd.md). That file contained extractable bill fields and line items in the body, enabling schema-compliant YAML frontmatter insertion without guessing. After successful structured-record maintenance, inbox-processing-v2 and 00_inbox rules required deleting the processed inbox file.
